ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
Off Calendar Mar-20-2023 Continued To May-08-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Russell Stanaland appeared by zoom for and with Michael Coholan in person.
Marlene Getchel appeared by zoom for and with Caroline Coholan by zoom.

Mrs. Getchel stated that the first accounting was never noticed, that she will be filing objections to the first and second accountings, that the accountings are deficient and that she is still asking the court to suspend Michael Coholan. The court noted that Mr. Coholan was ordered to make distribution by the end of the year (2022), and he didn't. Mr. Stanaland stated that his client would like the court to hold off until the April 11th hearing. Mr. Coholan stated that there are several small stocks holdings, that there were issues with getting the paper certificates, that the entire estate is now distributed, and that $121,000.00 is held in reserve for legal fees and taxes. Mr. Coholan also stated that they will have a 2023 return, and spoke of the total amounts that have been distributed to each of the beneficiaries. Mrs. Getchel stated that the accounting does not include the $30,000.00 debt that Michael owes to the trust, that they are concerned he will use the $121,000.00 for legal fees and for trustee's fees, that they are concerned that the reserve will be completely wiped out, and that this is her seventh appearance. The court stated that it will not suspend Michael Coholan today, that Mr. Coholan has made great stride getting the accountings filed and making distributions, and that the trustee's fees are up for review. The Petition for Approval of the First Accounting, etc., and the Petition for Orders for a Verified Accounting and Report, etc. are continued to May 8, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing on the current trustee's possible suspension is off calendar. The hearing date of April 11, 2023 on the second accounting is also continued to May 8, 2023. Objections are to be filed by April 26, 2023. The court orders Michael Coholan not to pay any attorney's fees or trustee's fees without a court order. Mr. Stanaland stated that he has no problem with holding off for court approval of his legal fees.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marlene Getchell, via Zoom, for and with Caroline Coholan, Petitioner; Russell Stanaland, via Zoom, for and with Michael Coholan, Petitioner.

Ms. Getchell reports of not having received a first accounting through April of 2021 nor has an accounting been filed. The court confirms with Mr. Stanaland that he intends to continue to represent Mr. Coholan in this matter and that an accounting has been filed. Mr. Coholan reports on the delay in preparing the accounting and that he believes his client should remain Trustee. The court and Ms. Getchell discuss whether she continues to believe that Mr. Coholan should be suspended. Ms. Getchell reminds the court of the delays with distributions in this matter and advises that she has no opposition to Mr. Coholan remaining as Trustee so long as the court orders for Mr. Coholan to meet deadlines on distributions by the end of December and for the filing of a second account to cover April of 2021 to the present with a filing deadline of February. Mr. Stanaland does not object to the deadlines proposed by Ms. Getchell specifically, as long as there is an understanding that distributions will be made with a reserve to cover possible taxes and reasonable expenses. The court orders that the next accounting filed by the Trustee include a full backup and fully complies with probate code. The court orders that distributions be made by the end of this month with a reserve as stated above.

All subject matters are continued to Feb-15-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Mr. Coholan is to file a second accounting as describe above by Feb-01-2023 and a Status Hearing thereon is also set for Feb-15-2023.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marlene Getchell, via Zoom, for and with Caroline Coholan, Petitioner; Russell Stanaland, via Zoom, for and with Michael Coholan.

Mr. Stanaland advises the court that while he is present today, his client has advised that he wishes to proceed with this hearing in propria persona. Ms. Getchell reports that she has not received any accounting and requests that the court remove Michael Coholan and appoint her client as successor Trustee. The court confirms with Mr. Coholan that he is searching for new counsel and requests a continuance accordingly. The court reminds Mr. Coholan that he was ordered at the last calling to provide an accounting. Mr. Coholan testifies as to the reasons he has been unable to provide the accounting and that he has submitted documents to an accounting firm and expects that he can file an accounting within two weeks. The court advises that it will not take the action requested by Ms. Getchell considering Mr. Coholan's legal status and intends to grant his request for a continuance with the caveat that if an accounting is not filed by the next calling, the court intends to suspend him and appoint Caroline Coholan as Trustee.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Dec-12-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A hearing on Michael Coholan's possible suspension is set for the same date. Michael Coholan is instructed to file an accounting by Dec-07-2022.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.

After additional discussion, the matters set for hearing today are continued to Jul-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Providing an Accounting to the Beneficiaries is set for the same date. Mr. Coholan is advised that he is to provide the accounting to the beneficiaries by Jul-11-2022.
